content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 16, 2003


Karin D. Troedsson
Deputy Town Attorney
c/o Coombs & Dunlap
1211 Division Street
Napa, CA 94559-3398

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-03-134

Dear Ms. Troedsson:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Councilmember
Donna Steiger regarding provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Does Councilmember Steiger have a real property interest, within the meaning of
the Act, in her residence at the Veterans Home of California at Yountville?

CONCLUSION

No. Individual residents of the Veterans Home of California do not acquire a real
property interest in the state-owned facility.

FACTS

Donna Steiger is a military veteran and a resident at the Veterans Home of
California ("VHC") at Yountville, which is owned and operated by the California
Department of Veterans Affairs. VHC provides Ms. Steiger with a semi-private room,
complete medical and dental care, and facilities that include a golf course, swimming
pool, library and theater. She pays member fees in the amount of $500 per month.

___________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-03-083
Page No. 2

To qualify for admittance to VHC, veterans must be aged or disabled residents of
California. They must also fulfill a number of other requirements including active duty
service in the armed forces, honorable discharge, and eligibility under the laws and
regulations of the United States Department of Veterans Affairs. You have supplied us
with a printed copy of an admissions application to VHC downloaded from its website,
and federal regulations describing admission, transfer and discharge rights at federal
facilities (38 CFR § 51.80), together with portions of the VHC Administrative Manual
governing the same topics. You have said in a telephone conversation that you have not
been able to obtain or review any documents executed when Ms. Steiger was admitted to
VHC, but you are not aware that they include a lease, or any transfer, understanding,
agreement, or waiver of real property rights by either VHC or Ms. Steiger.

Yountville prepares a conflict of interest map for all council members to assist
them in identifying properties within 500 feet of their real property interests. Ms. Steiger
was recently elected to the town council, and the town is now generating a conflict of
interest map for her.

ANALYSIS

An economic interest in real property, which may give rise to a conflict of interest
under the Act, is defined at § 82033 to include all forms of legal or equitable ownership
rights in any parcel of real property. Such rights commonly originate in purchase or lease
agreements, or under the terms of a trust. You have no reason to believe that Ms. Steiger
has acquired a real property interest in her residence at VHC through such conventional
means. Instead, you ask whether her occupation of a semi-private room at VHC, under
the terms of California Military and Veteran's Code, creates an interest in real property
for purposes of the Act's conflict of interest provisions. The Commission does not act as
a finder of fact when it offers advice or assistance, and our response is therefore limited
to the facts you have provided to us.² As we understand your question, you are asking
whether admission and residency at VHC, by itself, provides the residents with a real
property interest in the facility.

The Commission does not interpret any body of law other than the Act, but its
staff may consult the plain meaning of other statutes or regulations when such a step is
required to interpret a term used by the Act. Pertinent law and implementing regulations
we reviewed contemplate cases where it will become necessary to discharge or transfer a
resident. The resident's enumerated rights do not include compensation for loss of a real
property interest in his or her room. We conclude from this that the State of California
does not grant to residents a real property interest in its facilities at VHC. Thus, for
purposes of the Act's conflict of interest provisions, Ms. Steiger does not have a real
property interest in any portion of VHC.

² Regulation 18329(b)(7); In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.

File No. A-03-083
Page No. 3

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Lawrence T. Woodlock
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

the Act, in her residence at the Veterans Home of California at yountville?

CONCLUSION: No. Individual residents of the Veterans Home of California do not acquire a real
property interest in the state-owned facility.
